name: BrightCart — AI Lead Qualification Bot
description: Webhook-triggered lead qualification pipeline. Scores inbound leads with OpenAI, logs every lead to Airtable, and alerts the team in Slack only when a lead qualifies as hot.

flow.notes: Entry point. Receives lead form submissions (name, email, company, message/inquiry) as JSON payload.

sample_input.name: Jordan Ellis
sample_input.message: We're doing about 400 orders/month and our current support setup can't keep up. Looking for something that scales with us in the next 30 days.

flow.notes: Sends the lead's message plus context fields to GPT with a structured prompt. The model returns a qualification score, category, and a suggested next action as its completion text.
flow.sample_output:
Score: 87
Category: Hot
Next Action: Reach out within the hour, this lead has clear volume and urgency signals.

flow.notes: Converts OpenAI's structured text completion into usable fields (score, category, next_action) for the downstream Airtable and Router modules. This step is what makes the AI's free-text output machine-readable for the rest of the scenario.

sample_output.next_action: Reach out within the hour, this lead has clear volume and urgency signals.

flow.notes: Logs every lead that comes through the webhook, regardless of score. Airtable is the system of record here, nothing gets lost even if it doesn't qualify as hot.
Splits the flow based on lead category. Only 'Hot' leads continue to the Slack alert path, this keeps the team's Slack channel from getting noisy on every form submission.

routes.label: (unbuilt — extension point)
routes.notes: Intentionally left open in this demo. In a production build, this path would typically route Warm/Cold leads into a nurture sequence (e.g., an automated follow-up email or a CRM tag for later outreach) rather than dropping them after the Airtable log.

flow.notes: Fires only for leads the Router classified as Hot. Keeps the sales team's attention on leads worth an immediate response instead of every inbound form submission.
flow.sample_message:
:fire: Hot lead: Jordan Ellis (Example Cart Co.)
Score: 87/100
Next action: Reach out within the hour, this lead has clear volume and urgency signals.
Email: jordan@examplecart.com

extension_points: Second Router path: route Warm/Cold leads into a nurture sequence instead of ending at the Airtable log.
Add a CRM module (e.g., HubSpot, GHL) alongside Airtable so the lead also lands directly in the sales pipeline.
Add a confidence/reasoning field to the OpenAI prompt so low-confidence scores can be flagged for manual review.
